zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Bridge於1668年9月9日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利時，他的父親John Bridge29歲，母親Prudence Robinson24歲。他於1690年5月27日與Mary Brooks在美國德拉瓦州劍橋大學默里 卡素結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1733年9月23日在他的家鄉享年65歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Bridge于1668年9月9日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利时，他的父亲John Bridge29岁，母亲Prudence Robinson24岁。他于1690年5月27日与Mary Brooks在美国特拉华州劍橋大學默里 卡素结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1733年9月23日在他的家乡享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bridge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English brigge ‘bridge’, Old English brycg, applied as a topographic name for someone who lived near a bridge, a metonymic occupational name for a bridge keeper, or a habitational name from any of the places called with this element, as for example Bridge in Kent or Bridge Sollers in Herefordshire. Building and maintaining bridges was one of the three main feudal obligations, along with bearing arms and maintaining fortifications. The cost of building a bridge was often defrayed by charging a toll, the surname thus being acquired by the toll gatherer.

variants.name: Bridges
meanings: English: variant of Bridge . The -s generally represents the genitive case, but may occasionally be a plural. In some cases this name denoted someone from the Flemish city of Bruges (Brugge) in Belgium, meaning ‘bridges’, which had extensive trading links with England in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Bridger
meanings: English: from an agent derivative of Middle English brigge, brugge ‘bridge’ (Old English brycg), denoting either someone who lived near a bridge or the keeper of a bridge. See Bridge .
Americanized form of German Brücker (see Brucker ).

variants.name: Burge
meanings: English: variant of Bridge , Old English brycg, with metathesis of u and r, as exemplified in several placenames of this origin in various parts of southern England.
Swiss German and German (Bürge): from Middle High German bürge ‘bailsman, guarantor’. Compare Buerge .

variants.name: Bridgeman
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name from Middle English brigge, brugge ‘bridge’ (Old English brycg) + man, denoting either a dweller by or a keeper of a bridge. See Bridge .
In some cases possibly also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German Bruckmann (see Bruckman ).
History: James Bridgeman or Bridgman (1620–76) came to Hartford, CT, from Winchester, Hampshire, England, in 1640.

variants.name: Brugman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Erna, Kurt.
Dutch: topographic name for someone who lived near a bridge or a metonymic occupational name for a bridge keeper, from Dutch brug ‘bridge’ (see Bridge ). Compare Bruggeman .
Americanized form of Swiss German Bruggmann ‘bridge keeper’.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Bridge was born on 9 September 1668, in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, his father, John Bridge, was 29 and his mother, Prudence Robinson, was 24. He married Mary Brooks on 27 May 1690, in New Castle, Delaware, United States. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 23 September 1733, in his hometown, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Bridge est né le 9 septembre 1668, à Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, John Bridge, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Prudence Robinson, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Mary Brooks le 27 mai 1690, à New Castle, Delaware, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 23 septembre 1733, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 65 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward Bridge am 9. September 1668 in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Bridge 29 und seine Mutter Prudence Robinson 24. Er hat am 27. Mai 1690 in New Castle, Delaware, Vereinigte Staaten Mary Brooks geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 23. September 1733 im Alter von 65 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Bridge è nato il il 9 settembre 1668 nel Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, il suo padre, John Bridge, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Prudence Robinson, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Mary Brooks il 27 maggio 1690, nel New Castle, Delaware, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 23 settembre 1733, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 65 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward Bridgeが1668年9月9日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクロクスブリーで生まれた時，父John Bridgeは29歳，母Prudence Robinsonは24歳であった。1690年5月27日にアメリカ合衆国デラウェア州ニュー カストルでMary Brooksと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1733年9月23日に故郷で65歳で死去した。

ko.Roxbury, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴 록스버이
ko.Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colo: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이
ko.Roxbury, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴 록스버이
ko.Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay, Bri: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이
ko.lifeSummary: Edward Bridge(이)가 1668년 9월 9일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Bridge(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Prudence Robinson(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1690년 5월 27일 미국 델라웨어 주 뉴 카스틀에서 Mary Brooks(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1733년 9월 23일 그의 고향에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Bridge nasceu em 9 de setembro de 1668, em Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, seu pai, John Bridge, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Prudence Robinson, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Brooks em 27 de maio de 1690, em New Castle, Delaware, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 23 de setembro de 1733, em sua cidade natal, com 65 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Bridge родился 9 сентября 1668 г. в местности роксбури, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени John Bridge было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Prudence Robinson, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Brooks 27 мая 1690 г. в местности ню кастл, Делавэр, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 6 дочерей. Он умер 23 сентября 1733 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 65 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Bridge nació el 9 de septiembre de 1668, en Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, John Bridge, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Prudence Robinson, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Mary Brooks el 27 de mayo de 1690, en New Castle, Delaware, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 23 de septiembre de 1733, en su pueblo, a la edad de 65 años.
